zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes de Turberville於大約1300年出生在威爾士格拉莫爾根可迪時，她的父親Pain de Turberville37歲，母親Gwenllian de Talbot31歲。她和John de La Bere至少有4個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於1360年12月在英國賀福爾斯特特福德享年61歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Turberville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name, probably from Thouberville (Eure), or possibly from Trubleville (Seine-Maritime), both in France. The two may have been confused in surname usage.

variants.name: Torbet
meanings: from the Anglo-Norman and Middle English personal name T(h)urbert, T(h)orbert, a hybrid of Old Norse Thorr ‘Thor’, the name of the god of thunder, + ancient Germanic -berht ‘bright, famous’. The name was probably formed on the Continent; see Turberville .
perhaps in some cases a nickname from Old French and Middle English turbot, turbut ‘turbot’, for someone who caught or sold or resembled this fish.

variants.name: Tuberville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Turberville  . This form of the surname is very rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Turville
meanings: English (Surrey and Hampshire; of Norman origin):
habitational name from Tourville‐la‐Campagne (Eure, France). The placename derives from the Old Norse personal name Thórr + Old French ville ‘farmstead, estate’ (from Latin villa).
occasionally a shortened form of   Turberville  .

variants.name: Troublefield
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Turberville  .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes de Turberville was born about 1300, in Coity, Glamorgan, Wales, her father, Pain de Turberville, was 37 and her mother, Gwenllian de Talbot, was 31. She had at least 4 sons and 3 daughters with John de La Bere. She died in December 1360, in Stretford, Herefordshire, England, at the age of 61.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes de Turbervilleが約1300年にウェールズグラモーガンコイトイで生まれた時，父Pain de Turbervilleは37歳，母Gwenllian de Talbotは31歳であった。彼女はJohn de La Bereとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1360年12月にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアストレトフォードで61歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes de Turberville(이)가 대략 1300년 웨일스 글램르건 코이티에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Pain de Turberville(은)는 37세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Gwenllian de Talbot(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 John de La Bere(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1360년 12월 영국 허퍼드실르 스트레트퍼드에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes de Turberville родилась около 1300 г. в местности коити, глэморган, Уэльс, её отцу по имени Pain de Turberville было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Gwenllian de Talbot, 31. У неё было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери от John de La Bere. Она умерла декабрь 1360 г. в местности стретфорд, хирефордшир, Англия в возрасте 61 год.
